Title: What is the difference between == and === operators in JavaScript?
Body:
I have been learning JavaScript and encountered both == and === operators. I understand that == checks for equality while === checks for strict equality, but I am confused about when to use each one.

For example:

console.log(1 == '1'); // true console.log(1 === '1'); // false

Can someone explain the type coercion rules that == follows? Are there any best practices for which one to use in production code?

Title: How to properly implement authentication with JWT tokens in a Node.js API? [456]
Body:
I am building a REST API with Node.js and Express, and I want to implement JWT-based authentication. I have a few concerns about security best practices:

1. Should I store the JWT in localStorage, sessionStorage, or an HTTP-only cookie?

2. How should I handle token refresh to maintain user sessions without forcing re-login?

3. What claims should I include in the payload?

Here is my current token generation:

const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken'); const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id }, process.env.SECRET, { expiresIn: '1h' });

Is a 1-hour expiration reasonable? How do I securely implement refresh tokens?

Title: Efficient way to find duplicates in a large list using Python?

duplicates = [] for i in range(len(data)): for j in range(i+1, len(data)): if data[i] == data[j]: duplicates.append(data[i])

I have tried adding the cors middleware to my Express server but the error persists. Here is my Express configuration:

What am I missing? Is there a way to configure CORS headers properly for both development and production environments?

Title: Efficient way to find duplicates in a large list using Python? - variant 80
Body:
I have a list of approximately 10 million strings and need to find all duplicate entries efficiently. My current approach uses a nested loop which has O(n^2) time complexity and takes too long.

Current approach:

duplicates = [] for i in range(len(data)): for j in range(i+1, len(data)): if data[i] == data[j]: duplicates.append(data[i])

I have considered using a set or dictionary for O(n) lookup. What would be the most memory-efficient approach for this scale of data? Should I consider using pandas or numpy for better performance?

Title: How to properly implement authentication with JWT tokens in a Node.js API?

I am building a REST API with Node.js and Express, and I want to implement JWT-based authentication. I have a few concerns about security best practices:

1. Should I store the JWT in localStorage, sessionStorage, or an HTTP-only cookie?

2. How should I handle token refresh to maintain user sessions without forcing re-login?

3. What claims should I include in the payload?

Here is my current token generation:

const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken'); const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id }, process.env.SECRET, { expiresIn: '1h' });

Is a 1-hour expiration reasonable? How do I securely implement refresh tokens?

Can someone explain the type coercion rules that == follows? Are there any best practices for which one to use in production code?

Title: Efficient way to find duplicates in a large list using Python?
Body:
I have a list of approximately 10 million strings and need to find all duplicate entries efficiently. My current approach uses a nested loop which has O(n^2) time complexity and takes too long.

Current approach:

duplicates = [] for i in range(len(data)): for j in range(i+1, len(data)): if data[i] == data[j]: duplicates.append(data[i])

I have considered using a set or dictionary for O(n) lookup. What would be the most memory-efficient approach for this scale of data? Should I consider using pandas or numpy for better performance?

Title: How does garbage collection work in Java and when does it run?
Body:
I am trying to understand Java's garbage collection mechanism. Specifically, I want to know:

1. How does the JVM decide which objects are eligible for garbage collection?

2. What are the different garbage collection algorithms available (Serial, Parallel, G1, ZGC)?

3. Can I force garbage collection to run, or is it always at the JVM's discretion?

I have read that calling System.gc() is just a suggestion to the JVM. In a production environment with high memory usage, what GC tuning parameters should I consider?

I am building a REST API with Node.js and Express, and I want to implement JWT-based authentication. I have a few concerns about security best practices:

1. Should I store the JWT in localStorage, sessionStorage, or an HTTP-only cookie?

2. How should I handle token refresh to maintain user sessions without forcing re-login?

3. What claims should I include in the payload?

Here is my current token generation:

const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken'); const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id }, process.env.SECRET, { expiresIn: '1h' });

Is a 1-hour expiration reasonable? How do I securely implement refresh tokens?

Title: How to handle CORS errors in a React app calling a REST API? [488]
Body:
I am building a React frontend that calls a Node.js Express backend API. When I make fetch requests from my React app (running on localhost:3000) to my API (running on localhost:5000), I get the following error:

Access to fetch at 'http://localhost:5000/api/data' from origin 'http://localhost:3000' has been blocked by CORS policy

I have tried adding the cors middleware to my Express server but the error persists. Here is my Express configuration:

const cors = require('cors'); app.use(cors());

What am I missing? Is there a way to configure CORS headers properly for both development and production environments?

Title: Efficient way to find duplicates in a large list using Python?
Body:
I have a list of approximately 10 million strings and need to find all duplicate entries efficiently. My current approach uses a nested loop which has O(n^2) time complexity and takes too long.

Current approach:

duplicates = [] for i in range(len(data)): for j in range(i+1, len(data)): if data[i] == data[j]: duplicates.append(data[i])

I have considered using a set or dictionary for O(n) lookup. What would be the most memory-efficient approach for this scale of data? Should I consider using pandas or numpy for better performance?

Title: How to properly implement authentication with JWT tokens in a Node.js API?
Body:
I am building a REST API with Node.js and Express, and I want to implement JWT-based authentication. I have a few concerns about security best practices:

1. Should I store the JWT in localStorage, sessionStorage, or an HTTP-only cookie?

2. How should I handle token refresh to maintain user sessions without forcing re-login?

3. What claims should I include in the payload?

Here is my current token generation:

const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken'); const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id }, process.env.SECRET, { expiresIn: '1h' });

Is a 1-hour expiration reasonable? How do I securely implement refresh tokens?

Title: How to properly implement authentication with JWT tokens in a Node.js API?
Body:
I am building a REST API with Node.js and Express, and I want to implement JWT-based authentication. I have a few concerns about security best practices:

1. Should I store the JWT in localStorage, sessionStorage, or an HTTP-only cookie?

2. How should I handle token refresh to maintain user sessions without forcing re-login?

3. What claims should I include in the payload?

Here is my current token generation:

const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken'); const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id }, process.env.SECRET, { expiresIn: '1h' });

Is a 1-hour expiration reasonable? How do I securely implement refresh tokens?

Title: How to handle CORS errors in a React app calling a REST API? (approach 139)
Body:

What am I missing? Is there a way to configure CORS headers properly for both development and production environments?

Title: How to properly implement authentication with JWT tokens in a Node.js API?
Body:
I am building a REST API with Node.js and Express, and I want to implement JWT-based authentication. I have a few concerns about security best practices:

1. Should I store the JWT in localStorage, sessionStorage, or an HTTP-only cookie?

2. How should I handle token refresh to maintain user sessions without forcing re-login?

3. What claims should I include in the payload?

Here is my current token generation:

const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken'); const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id }, process.env.SECRET, { expiresIn: '1h' });

Is a 1-hour expiration reasonable? How do I securely implement refresh tokens?

I have been learning JavaScript and encountered both == and === operators. I understand that == checks for equality while === checks for strict equality, but I am confused about when to use each one.

For example:

console.log(1 == '1'); // true console.log(1 === '1'); // false

Can someone explain the type coercion rules that == follows? Are there any best practices for which one to use in production code?

Title: Best practices for database indexing in PostgreSQL for query optimization? [299]
Body:
I have a PostgreSQL database with a table containing about 50 million rows. My queries are becoming slow, especially those with WHERE clauses on multiple columns and JOIN operations.

Current table structure:

CREATE TABLE orders ( id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, customer_id INT, product_id INT, order_date TIMESTAMP, total_amount DECIMAL(10,2), status VARCHAR(20) );

I frequently query by customer_id, order_date ranges, and status. Should I create composite indexes? What is the impact on INSERT performance? How do I use EXPLAIN ANALYZE to identify bottlenecks?

Title: Understanding Python decorators with arguments - how do they work internally?
Body:
I understand basic Python decorators, but I am struggling with decorators that accept arguments. For example:

@my_decorator(arg1, arg2) def my_function(): pass

This seems to involve three levels of nested functions. Can someone break down the execution flow step by step? How does Python handle the difference between @my_decorator and @my_decorator(args)?

I am particularly confused about how functools.wraps plays into this and why it is recommended. A clear example with the execution order would be very helpful.

duplicates = [] for i in range(len(data)): for j in range(i+1, len(data)): if data[i] == data[j]: duplicates.append(data[i])

Title: How to handle CORS errors in a React app calling a REST API? [319]
Body:
I am building a React frontend that calls a Node.js Express backend API. When I make fetch requests from my React app (running on localhost:3000) to my API (running on localhost:5000), I get the following error:

Access to fetch at 'http://localhost:5000/api/data' from origin 'http://localhost:3000' has been blocked by CORS policy

I have tried adding the cors middleware to my Express server but the error persists. Here is my Express configuration:

const cors = require('cors'); app.use(cors());

What am I missing? Is there a way to configure CORS headers properly for both development and production environments?

Title: How to properly implement authentication with JWT tokens in a Node.js API? [63]
Body:
I am building a REST API with Node.js and Express, and I want to implement JWT-based authentication. I have a few concerns about security best practices:

1. Should I store the JWT in localStorage, sessionStorage, or an HTTP-only cookie?

2. How should I handle token refresh to maintain user sessions without forcing re-login?

3. What claims should I include in the payload?

Here is my current token generation:

const jwt = require('jsonwebtoken'); const token = jwt.sign({ userId: user.id }, process.env.SECRET, { expiresIn: '1h' });

Is a 1-hour expiration reasonable? How do I securely implement refresh tokens?

Title: How to implement a binary search tree in Python with O(log n) lookup? [158]
Body:

class Node: def __init__(self, key): self.left = None self.right = None self.val = key
